---
title: "Sally Tsai"
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 15em
  image-shape: round
  image: photos\Canon-0806.JPG
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: "Email"
      href: "mailto:sallytsai6640@gmail.com"
    - icon: github
      text: "GitHub"
      href: https://github.com/timchen0326
    - icon: linkedin
      text: "LinkedIn"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sallyytsai/
---

I am a **Master in Public Administration in International Development (MPA/ID)** student at the **Harvard Kennedy School**. My work sits at the intersection of **quantitative analysis**, **development economics**, and **public policy**, with a focus on how careful evidence can inform better decisions for governments and global organizations.

In the MPA/ID program I study **microeconomics**, **macroeconomics**, **statistics**, and **program evaluation**, and I apply these tools to concrete policy questions. I am especially interested in topics such as inclusive growth, social protection, and state capacity in emerging and developing economies.

I have experience with **data analysis and statistical programming**, including applied coursework and research that use empirical tools to study policy problems. I enjoy collaborating in teams that combine technical skills with local knowledge and policy experience.

At Harvard Kennedy School I am also involved in the student community as part of international and development focused networks, and I care about creating spaces where students from different backgrounds can learn from one another.

Over the long term I hope to contribute to public sector and multilateral work that relies on sound economic reasoning, clear communication, and practical implementation to improve outcomes for people in low and middle income countries.
